varieties.name: Compass
varieties.traits: Spring barley, two-row malting; Medium height, vigorous, erect plant type
varieties.availability: AGF Seeds, Hart Bros Seeds, major Australian seed suppliers

varieties.name: Maximus CL
varieties.traits: Spring barley, Clearfield herbicide tolerant, malt accredited; Quick-mid maturity, erect plant type, excellent grain plumpness
varieties.yield_notes: Very high yielding, outperforms Spartacus CL
varieties.growing_notes: Improved disease resistance over Spartacus CL, CCN resistant

varieties.name: RGT Planet
varieties.traits: Spring barley, malt accredited; Mid-late maturity, strong agronomic package
varieties.growing_notes: Good resistance to mildew, net blotch, brown rust

varieties.name: Spartacus CL
varieties.traits: Spring barley, Clearfield herbicide tolerant, malt accredited; Quick maturity, strong lodging tolerance
varieties.availability: InterGrain (declining availability as Maximus CL replaces it)

varieties.name: La Trobe
varieties.traits: Spring barley, malt accredited; Consistent performer across varied seasons

varieties.name: Rosalind
varieties.traits: Spring barley, feed variety; Quick-mid maturity

varieties.name: Beast
varieties.traits: Spring barley, pending malt accreditation; Vigorous 'Compass' style plant type, suited to lower rainfall/Mallee environments

varieties.name: Cyclops
varieties.traits: Spring barley, pending malt accreditation; Broad regional adaptation across WA, SA, Vic, southern NSW
varieties.yield_notes: Exceptional performance across varied seasonal conditions

varieties.name: Fathom
varieties.traits: Spring barley, feed variety; Consistent performer

varieties.name: Schooner
varieties.traits: Spring barley, malting variety; Suitable for small-scale/home cultivation
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yields, suitable for backyard growing

varieties.name: Maris Otter
varieties.traits: Winter barley, two-row, heritage malting variety; Low protein, rich nutty flavor, biscuity character. Highly regarded in brewing for over 55 years
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate by modern standards, but prized for flavor
varieties.growing_notes: Susceptible to modern diseases, requires fungicide management
varieties.availability: Specialty malt suppliers, Crisp Malt, Thomas Fawcett, Muntons (primarily as malt)

varieties.name: Golden Promise
varieties.traits: Spring barley, heritage malting variety; Short, stiff straw, very early maturity (3 weeks before modern varieties), sweet clean flavor. First variety protected under UK Plant Varieties Act 1964
varieties.yield_notes: Lower than modern varieties, but flavor-focused

varieties.name: Chevallier
varieties.traits: Landrace heritage barley, first modern malting variety; Ancestral to modern malting varieties. Nutty, bready, marmalade flavors. Bold character

varieties.name: Plumage Archer
varieties.traits: Heritage malting barley; Cinder toffee character, brings out warm fireside character in dark beers

varieties.name: Scots Bere (Bere Barley)
varieties.origin: Scotland/Orkney Islands, United Kingdom, 8th-9th century (Viking introduction)
varieties.traits: Six-row landrace, ancient heritage variety; Hardy, adapted to short growing seasons, cool coastal climates, and poor soils. Low pH tolerance
varieties.yield_notes: Low by modern standards, but resilient
varieties.availability: Salt Spring Seeds (Canada), specialty heritage grain suppliers, Columbia Heritage Grain & Trading Company

varieties.name: Purple Hulless Barley
varieties.origin: Tibet, introduced to USA ~100 years ago, rediscovered from seed vaults
varieties.traits: Hulless variety, heirloom grain; Beautiful purple color, nutty flavor, hearty texture. Hull naturally falls during harvest leaving bran and germ intact

varieties.name: DWRUB52
varieties.origin: ICAR-Indian Institute of Wheat and Barley Research (IIWBR), India
varieties.traits: Malting barley, six-row; Adapted to subtropical climates with shorter grain filling windows
varieties.availability: National Seed Corporation (NSC), State Seed Farm Corporation, agricultural universities

varieties.name: DWRB101
varieties.traits: Malting barley; Suitable for malting in subtropical environments

varieties.name: RD2849
varieties.traits: Malting barley, six-row; Suitable for subtropical malting

varieties.name: DWRUB64
varieties.traits: Malting barley; Suitable for malting

varieties.name: DWRB91
varieties.traits: Malting barley; Suitable for subtropical malting conditions

varieties.name: BH-75 🏆
varieties.traits: Malting barley, semi-dwarf, early maturing, traditional variety; Semi-dwarf, early maturity, resistant to yellow rust. Used for malting purpose
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate by modern standards, but reliable

varieties.name: PL-426 🏆
varieties.traits: Malting barley, six-row, semi-dwarf, traditional variety; Semi-dwarf, matures in ~125 days, resistant to lodging, yellow rust, loose and covered smuts. Bold grains with thin husk

varieties.name: BH-393 🏆
varieties.traits: Malting barley, traditional variety; Suitable for Punjab and Haryana, sown under irrigated and timely conditions

varieties.name: RD2503
varieties.traits: Feed/Malt barley, six-row; Dual-purpose variety for feed and malt

varieties.name: RD2035
varieties.traits: Feed barley, irrigated; Developed for irrigated conditions

varieties.name: K551
varieties.traits: Malting barley; Developed for UP conditions

varieties.name: DWRB92
varieties.traits: Malt barley, high-yielding; Developed for North Western Plains Zone
varieties.yield_notes: Very high yields - 49.8 q/ha in NW Plains

varieties.name: BH946
varieties.traits: Feed barley, high-yielding; Developed for North Western Plains Zone
varieties.yield_notes: Very high yields - 52 q/ha in NW Plains

varieties.name: Narendra Barley-2 (NDB940)
varieties.origin: Narendra Deva University of Agriculture and Technology, Uttar Pradesh, India
varieties.traits: Feed barley, irrigated; Developed for eastern UP region

varieties.name: Geetanjali (K1149)
varieties.traits: Feed barley, rainfed; Developed for rainfed/dryland conditions

varieties.name: PL172 🏆
varieties.traits: Traditional six-row semi-dwarf barley, irrigated; Resistant to lodging, dense tapered ears, bold white grains with thin husk

varieties.name: DWRB223
varieties.traits: First hulless barley variety in India; Hulless - absence of husk makes it directly consumable, valuable for health foods

varieties.name: Azad (K-125) 🏆
varieties.traits: Traditional feed barley; Old variety still grown by some farmers

varieties.name: Jagriti 🏆
varieties.traits: Traditional variety; Old variety with low yield

varieties.name: Manjula 🏆
varieties.traits: Traditional variety; Old variety with traditional characteristics

varieties.name: RD2786
varieties.traits: Feed barley, central zone variety; Developed for central India conditions
varieties.yield_notes: 50.20 qtl/ha average, 15.70% higher than central zone check

varieties.name: RD2794
varieties.traits: Feed barley; Developed for central zone

varieties.name: VLB 118 (VL JAU 118)
varieties.origin: Vivekananda Parvatiya Krishi Anusandhan Sansthan, Almora, Uttarakhand, India
varieties.traits: Feed barley, hill variety; Developed for northern hills rainfed conditions

varieties.name: Pusa Sheetal (BHS 400)
varieties.traits: Variety for north India; Developed for cooler northern regions

varieties.name: Ratna
varieties.traits: Traditional variety; Older variety
